macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Markets Close Mixed as Inflation Data Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: The S&P 500 and NASDAQ slipped from recent record highs after hotter-than-expected CPI data raised concerns about persistent inflation. The Nasdaq fell 186 points while the Dow Jones gained 56 points, supported by healthcare shares.

items.title: Fed Rate Cut Expectations Diminish Amid Stubborn Inflation
items.summary: Fresh U.S. consumer inflation data exceeded forecasts, reinforcing concerns that the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts or consider additional tightening if inflationary pressures persist. The 10-year yield moved closer to 4.45%.

items.title: Wholesale Prices Jump 6% Year-Over-Year, Largest Increase Since 2022
items.summary: New data reveals wholesale prices have surged 6 percent compared to last year, marking the biggest jump since 2022 and adding to inflation concerns across the economy.

items.title: Kevin Warsh Advances Toward Fed Chair Confirmation
items.summary: President Trump's Fed Chair pick Kevin Warsh cleared a key Senate vote and is now one step closer to becoming Federal Reserve chairman, set to replace Jerome Powell.

items.title: Technology Stocks Lose Momentum as Investors Book Profits
items.summary: The AI-driven rally in semiconductor and chip companies lost momentum as investors booked profits amid market uncertainty and inflation concerns.

items.title: Oil Prices Remain Elevated at $106 Brent Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Brent crude oil remains elevated at $106 per barrel as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to pressure global energy supplies.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above 200-Day Moving Average Amid Market Maturation
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading at $80,275, breaking above its 200-day moving average—a technical signal last seen during previous bull markets. The breakout reflects increased institutional adoption and clearer regulatory frameworks, though analysts caution that the signal alone doesn't guarantee an immediate rally.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $125M in 24 Hours
items.summary: Over $125 million in cryptocurrency futures were liquidated in 24 hours, with Bitcoin accounting for $58.02M (77% longs) and Ethereum $56.52M (86.61% longs). The concentrated long liquidations indicate a leverage-driven reset that increased short-term volatility and downside risk.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $80K Despite Hot US Inflation Data
items.summary: Bitcoin maintained above the $80,000 mark despite hotter-than-expected U.S. CPI data, trading at $81,117 and signaling strong buyer confidence. On-chain activity and institutional inflows remained active despite macroeconomic uncertainty.

items.title: Charles Schwab Launches Direct Spot Crypto Trading for $12 Trillion in Client Assets
items.summary: Charles Schwab has begun rolling out direct Bitcoin and Ether spot trading to eligible retail clients, marking a significant Wall Street entry point for crypto since spot Bitcoin ETF approvals. The service is live in most U.S. states, excluding New York and Louisiana initially.

items.title: Cosmos (ATOM) Surges 9.5% But May Be Forming Bull Trap
items.summary: ATOM rallied 9.5% but market analysis suggests the rally could be a bull trap, with rising short positions, spot outflows of $374K over 40 days, and liquidation clusters indicating sellers still dominate. Perpetual market volume surged to $250M with shorts leading trades.

items.title: Bitcoin at Crossroads: Over $400M Liquidations as Regulatory Clarity Emerges
items.summary: Bitcoin experienced over $400M in liquidations within 24 hours while testing critical resistance levels. Expert forecasts suggest Bitcoin could reach $100,000 to $230,000 in 2026, driven by institutional adoption and potential regulatory clarity from the CLARITY Act.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: PlayStation Plus May 2026 monthly games reportedly include EA Sports FC 26, Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, and Nine Sols
items.summary: A recent PlayStation-focused video says the PlayStation Plus May 2026 monthly lineup has been revealed, highlighting EA Sports FC 26, Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, and Nine Sols. The video also notes that players can claim the titles now.

items.title: IO Interactive says 007 First Light runs at 60 FPS on PS5 Pro
items.summary: IO Interactive has reportedly confirmed that 007 First Light is running at 60 frames per second on PS5 Pro, responding to criticism about the game's performance. The title is said to be scheduled for release on May 27.

items.title: SCUF Omega officially licensed PS5 controller launches
items.summary: A new officially licensed SCUF Omega PS5 performance controller has launched, with the video describing it as a feature-packed release for competitive players. It appears to be one of the main PlayStation hardware announcements in the last day.

items.title: The Division 2 fans react to the return of the Caduceus Exotic Assault Rifle
items.summary: A new The Division 2 update has reportedly brought back the Caduceus Exotic Assault Rifle, sparking excitement among veteran players. The video says the return has already generated strong community interest.

items.title: Old School RuneScape players react to new Gem Bag update
items.summary: A RuneScape news roundup says Jagex has dropped a new Gem Bag, with players discussing its impact on in-game money-making and item prices. The update appears to be part of broader OSRS and RS3 news coverage.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony's Xperia 1 VIII Arrives with New Design and Premium Pricing
items.summary: Sony has launched the Xperia 1 VIII smartphone featuring bigger camera sensors and a redesigned form factor, though the device will not be released in the US market.

items.title: Samsung Launches One UI 9 Beta with Android 17 for Galaxy S26
items.summary: Samsung has released the One UI 9 beta update bringing Android 17 to the Galaxy S26 across six markets, with the first beta now available for download.

items.title: iOS 26.5 Introduces Cross-Platform Encrypted RCS Messaging
items.summary: Apple's iOS 26.5 update brings default end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging between iPhone and Android devices, though with some limitations.

items.title: eBay Rejects GameStop's $56 Billion Unsolicited Acquisition Offer
items.summary: eBay has formally rejected GameStop's unsolicited bid to acquire the company for $56 billion, calling the offer 'neither credible nor attractive.'

items.title: AWS US-East-1 Region Experiences Outage Due to Data Center Cooling Failure
items.summary: A thermal event and cooling system failure at AWS's primary US-East-1 data center caused server overheating and power downs, impacting EC2 and EBS services.

items.title: Oppo Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s Coming to India Next Week
items.summary: Oppo is preparing to launch its Find X9 Ultra and Find X9s flagship smartphones in the Indian market.
